entry.prompt: Express GFP from a constitutive promoter in E. coli
Express luciferase from a T7 promoter in E. coli
Constitutive YFP expression with the B0034 RBS in E. coli
Express mCherry from the GAL1 promoter in S. cerevisiae (galactose-induced)
Constitutive mCherry from the EF1a promoter in HEK293 cells
Express GUS reporter under a ubiquitin promoter in tobacco
Simple His-tagged mRFP expression from a constitutive promoter in E. coli
aTc-inducible GFP using TetR repression of pTet in E. coli
IPTG-inducible expression of a T7 RNA polymerase, which then drives sfGFP from a T7 promoter
Create an aTc-inducible RFP reporter with a TetR repression module
Copper-inducible GFP using the CUP1 promoter in S. cerevisiae
Dexamethasone-inducible GFP using the GVG system in Arabidopsis
Zinc biosensor using ZntR activation of PzntA driving mCherry
Heat-shock responsive GFP using sigma-32 (rpoH) driven PibpA promoter
NOT gate using PhlF repressor: constitutive PhlF represses pPhlF driving GFP; DAPG de-represses
IMPLIES gate using LacI and TetR: output ON unless LacI ON and TetR OFF

response:
{
  "name": "implies_gate_lac_tet",
  "components": [
    {"name": "p_lac", "type": "promoter", "description": "Lactose-inducible promoter driving LacI expression"},
    {"name": "rbs_lac", "type": "rbs", "description": "RBS for LacI translation"},
    {"name": "laci", "type": "cds", "description": "LacI repressor protein"},
    {"name": "t_lac", "type": "terminator", "description": "Transcription terminator for LacI unit"},
    {"name": "op_lac", "type": "operator", "description": "Lac operator site within p_lac"},
    {"name": "p_tet", "type": "promoter", "description": "Tetracycline-inducible promoter driving TetR expression"},
    {"name": "rbs_tet", "type": "rbs", "description": "RBS for TetR translation"},
    {"name": "tetr", "type": "cds", "description": "TetR repressor protein"},
    {"name": "t_tet", "type": "terminator", "description": "Transcription terminator for TetR unit"},
    {"name": "op_tet", "type": "operator", "description": "Tet operator site within p_tet"},
    {"name": "p_dual", "type": "promoter", "description": "Output promoter containing both Lac and Tet operator sites"},
    {"name": "rbs_gfp", "type": "rbs", "description": "RBS for GFP translation"},
    {"name": "gfp", "type": "cds", "description": "Green Fluorescent Protein reporter"},
    {"name": "t_gfp", "type": "terminator", "description": "Transcription terminator for GFP unit"},
    {"name": "op_dual_lac", "type": "operator", "description": "Lac operator site within the dual output promoter"},
    {"name": "op_dual_tet", "type": "operator", "description": "Tet operator site within the dual output promoter"}
  ],
  "interactions": [
    {"from": "p_lac", "to": "laci", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_lac", "to": "laci", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "p_tet", "to": "tetr", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_tet", "to": "tetr", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "p_dual", "to": "gfp", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_gfp", "to": "gfp", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "laci", "to": "op_lac", "type": "repression"},
    {"from": "tetr", "to": "op_tet", "type": "repression"},
    {"from": "laci", "to": "op_dual_lac", "type": "repression"},
    {"from": "tetr", "to": "op_dual_tet", "type": "repression"}
  ],
  "behavior": "This circuit implements an IMPLIES logic gate (LacI -> TetR) where the output (GFP) is ON unless LacI is present and TetR is absent. The output promoter p_dual contains both Lac and Tet operators; it is repressed by LacI (if induced) and by TetR (if induced). If LacI is ON (repressing output) and TetR is OFF (not repressing), the output is OFF. In all other states (LacI OFF, or TetR ON), the repression logic ensures the output is either fully active or partially active depending on the specific operator affinities, but the prompt specifies 'ON unless LacI ON and TetR OFF', which corresponds to the logical implication A -> B being false only when A is true and B is false. Here, LacI presence (A) represses, and TetR absence (not B) fails to repress, resulting in the OFF state.",
  "organism": "Escherichia coli"
}

entry.prompt: CRISPRi-based toggle: two sgRNAs cross-repressing each other's promoters
Mammalian toggle switch between two states using TetR and PipR with CMV-driven readouts
NOR gate using two tandem repressor binding sites driving GFP
Ratio-sensor: output GFP proportional to ratio of AraC/LacI activation
Elowitz repressilator: three mutually repressing repressors (LacI, TetR, CI) with GFP readout

response:
{
  "name": "elowitz_repressilator",
  "components": [
    {"name": "p_lac", "type": "promoter", "description": "LacI-repressible promoter driving tetR expression"},
    {"name": "op_lac", "type": "operator", "description": "Lac operator site within p_lac"},
    {"name": "rbs_tetr", "type": "rbs", "description": "Ribosome binding site for tetR translation"},
    {"name": "tetr", "type": "cds", "description": "TetR repressor protein"},
    {"name": "t_tetr", "type": "terminator", "description": "Transcription terminator for tetR"},
    {"name": "p_tet", "type": "promoter", "description": "TetR-repressible promoter driving lambda CI expression"},
    {"name": "op_tet", "type": "operator", "description": "Tet operator site within p_tet"},
    {"name": "rbs_ci", "type": "rbs", "description": "Ribosome binding site for lambda CI translation"},
    {"name": "ci", "type": "cds", "description": "Lambda CI repressor protein"},
    {"name": "t_ci", "type": "terminator", "description": "Transcription terminator for lambda CI"},
    {"name": "p_ci", "type": "promoter", "description": "CI-repressible promoter driving lacI expression"},
    {"name": "op_ci", "type": "operator", "description": "CI operator site within p_ci"},
    {"name": "rbs_laci", "type": "rbs", "description": "Ribosome binding site for lacI translation"},
    {"name": "laci", "type": "cds", "description": "LacI repressor protein"},
    {"name": "t_laci", "type": "terminator", "description": "Transcription terminator for lacI"},
    {"name": "p_const", "type": "promoter", "description": "Constitutive promoter driving gfp expression"},
    {"name": "rbs_gfp", "type": "rbs", "description": "Ribosome binding site for gfp translation"},
    {"name": "gfp", "type": "cds", "description": "Green fluorescent protein reporter"},
    {"name": "t_gfp", "type": "terminator", "description": "Transcription terminator for gfp"}
  ],
  "interactions": [
    {"from": "p_lac", "to": "tetr", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_tetr", "to": "tetr", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "p_tet", "to": "ci", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_ci", "to": "ci", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "p_ci", "to": "laci", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_laci", "to": "laci", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "p_const", "to": "gfp", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_gfp", "to": "gfp", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "laci", "to": "op_lac", "type": "repression"},
    {"from": "tetr", "to": "op_tet", "type": "repression"},
    {"from": "ci", "to": "op_ci", "type": "repression"}
  ],
  "behavior": "This circuit implements a three-node negative feedback loop where LacI represses TetR, TetR represses Lambda CI, and Lambda CI represses LacI. The cyclic repression creates a delay that drives sustained oscillations in the concentration of each repressor protein. A constitutive GFP reporter allows visualization of these oscillations as periodic fluorescence changes in the population.",
  "organism": "Escherichia coli"
}

entry.prompt: Copper biosensor: CusR activation of PcusC drives GFP; CueR as orthogonal control
Negative autoregulation of TetR reducing noise in downstream GFP
pH biosensor based on CadC-activated CadA promoter driving mCherry
Glucose biosensor in HEK293 using a ChREBP-based synthetic TF activating luciferase
Incoherent feedforward loop: input activates Y and Z, Y represses Z — pulse generator for GFP

response:
{
  "name": "incoherent_feedforward_pulse_generator",
  "components": [
    {"name": "p_tet", "type": "promoter", "description": "Tetracycline-inducible promoter driving the input sensor"},
    {"name": "rbs_lac_i", "type": "rbs", "description": "Ribosome binding site for LacI translation"},
    {"name": "lac_i", "type": "cds", "description": "LacI repressor protein, activated by input signal"},
    {"name": "t_rrnb", "type": "terminator", "description": "Strong transcriptional terminator for LacI"},
    {"name": "p_lac", "type": "promoter", "description": "LacI-repressible promoter driving the output reporter"},
    {"name": "rbs_gfp", "type": "rbs", "description": "Ribosome binding site for GFP translation"},
    {"name": "gfp", "type": "cds", "description": "Green fluorescent protein reporter"},
    {"name": "t_gfp", "type": "terminator", "description": "Transcriptional terminator for GFP"},
    {"name": "op_lac", "type": "operator", "description": "Lac operator site within p_lac for LacI binding"},
    {"name": "p_j23101", "type": "promoter", "description": "Constitutive promoter driving the output reporter (parallel arm)"},
    {"name": "rbs_gfp2", "type": "rbs", "description": "Ribosome binding site for the second GFP copy"},
    {"name": "gfp2", "type": "cds", "description": "Green fluorescent protein reporter (direct arm)"},
    {"name": "t_gfp2", "type": "terminator", "description": "Transcriptional terminator for the second GFP copy"}
  ],
  "interactions": [
    {"from": "p_tet", "to": "lac_i", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_lac_i", "to": "lac_i", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "p_lac", "to": "gfp", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_gfp", "to": "gfp", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "p_j23101", "to": "gfp2", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_gfp2", "to": "gfp2", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "lac_i", "to": "op_lac", "type": "repression"},
    {"from": "lac_i", "to": "p_lac", "type": "repression"}
  ],
  "behavior": "This circuit implements an incoherent feedforward loop where the input (tetracycline) induces LacI expression. LacI directly activates a constitutive GFP output (via the p_j23101 arm in a real biological context, though here modeled as a parallel constitutive output for the 'activation' arm logic) while simultaneously repressing a second GFP output driven by p_lac. The net result is a transient pulse of GFP expression: the direct activation rises quickly, while the delayed repression of the second arm creates a peak followed by a decline as the repressor accumulates.",
  "organism": "Escherichia coli"
}

entry.prompt: Hypoxia biosensor: FNR-activated PnarK drives GFP only under anaerobic conditions
Beta-carotene production pathway: CrtE, CrtB, CrtI, CrtY expressed from pBAD arabinose-inducible operon

response:
{
  "name": "beta_carotene_production_pathway",
  "components": [
    {"name": "pbad", "type": "promoter", "description": "Arabinose-inducible promoter driving the operon"},
    {"name": "rbs_crt", "type": "rbs", "description": "Strong RBS for the beta-carotene pathway operon"},
    {"name": "crt_e", "type": "cds", "description": "Geranylgeranyl diphosphate synthase"},
    {"name": "crt_b", "type": "cds", "description": "Phytoene synthase"},
    {"name": "crt_i", "type": "cds", "description": "Phytoene desaturase"},
    {"name": "crt_y", "type": "cds", "description": "Lycopene cyclase"},
    {"name": "rrnb_terminator", "type": "terminator", "description": "Strong transcriptional terminator for the operon"},
    {"name": "beta_carotene", "type": "other", "description": "Final metabolic product beta-carotene"}
  ],
  "interactions": [
    {"from": "pbad", "to": "crt_e", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_crt", "to": "crt_e", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "pbad", "to": "crt_b", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_crt", "to": "crt_b", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "pbad", "to": "crt_i", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_crt", "to": "crt_i", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "pbad", "to": "crt_y", "type": "transcription"},
    {"from": "rbs_crt", "to": "crt_y", "type": "translation"},
    {"from": "crt_e", "to": "beta_carotene", "type": "production"},
    {"from": "crt_b", "to": "beta_carotene", "type": "production"},
    {"from": "crt_i", "to": "beta_carotene", "type": "production"},
    {"from": "crt_y", "to": "beta_carotene", "type": "production"}
  ],
  "behavior": "This circuit implements a metabolic pathway for beta-carotene biosynthesis in Escherichia coli. Upon addition of arabinose, the pBAD promoter activates transcription of a polycistronic operon containing CrtE, CrtB, CrtI, and CrtY. These enzymes function sequentially to convert endogenous precursors into beta-carotene, with the pathway logic emerging from the coordinated expression of all four enzymes under a single inducible promoter.",
  "organism": "Escherichia coli"
}

entry.prompt: CRISPRa activator using dCas9-VP64 with sgRNA recruiting a synthetic activator to upregulate endogenous gene
Dual-input kill switch in E. coli: without both IPTG and arabinose, CcdB toxin kills cells; both inducers maintain CcdA antitoxin
Engineer a tumor-targeting E. coli Nissle circuit: hypoxia-responsive FNR promoter drives InvA; constitutive anti-HER2 nanobody surface-displayed via INP anchor
Yeast galactose utilization rewiring: GAL80 knockout with GAL4-VP64 driving constitutive GAL1/7/10 expression
CRISPR-Cas13-based RNA sensor: Cas13 cleaves a quenched GFP mRNA when trigger RNA is present
Cell-free CRISPR sensor: Cas12a activates with trigger DNA, cleaving a quenched fluorescent reporter in a TX-TL reaction
